title: Magic States and Quantum Computation
description: Master the resource theory of magic - understanding non-stabilizer states as computational resources, stabilizer formalism, magic state distillation, and the connection to fault-tolerant quantum computing.
learningObjectives: Define stabilizer states and understand why they form the free states for magic
Calculate magic measures including robustness and mana
Understand the Gottesman-Knill theorem and its implications
Connect magic to universal quantum computation
Apply magic theory to fault-tolerant quantum computing design
